ontology.foodex2_description: Cheese, processed, fried — closest parent category for breaded fried mozzarella sticks
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists specifically for breaded fried mozzarella sticks; A0CJW (processed cheese product) used as closest parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT food concept identified for breaded fried mozzarella sticks.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of mozzarella cheese sticks is 4 pieces, weighing approximately 112 g, as sold by Now Pizza at Funan, Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Mozzarella Sticks, breaded, fried — USDA FoodData Central, SR Legacy / Foundation Foods
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values derived from USDA FoodData Central data for breaded fried mozzarella sticks (SR Legacy reference item: 'Cheese, mozzarella sticks, breaded, fried'). Values represent the composite of mozzarella cheese core and wheat-flour breadcrumb coating after deep-frying. Per-100g values calculated from USDA reported data. Serving size of 4 pieces (~112 g) is consistent with typical restaurant portions.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for breaded fried mozzarella sticks. GI estimated at 35 based on analogous breaded fried cheese products and the known low GI of mozzarella cheese (GI ~0–5 for plain cheese) combined with the moderate GI contribution of the wheat-flour breadcrumb coating. The composite GI of ~35 is consistent with similar breaded fried snacks reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care). GL per serving = (35 × 22.4 × 1.12) / 100 = 8.8 g. gi_category set to 'low' per threshold rule (GI ≤ 55).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (512 mg per 100 g; approximately 574 mg per 4-piece serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid mozzarella cheese sticks due to the high sodium content from both the cheese and seasoned breadcrumb coating, which can contribute significantly to daily sodium targets of under 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate load from breadcrumb coating with low glycemic index (GI ~35); high saturated fat content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Mozzarella cheese sticks have a low GI (~35) due to the protein and fat content of the cheese, but the breaded coating contributes a moderate glycemic load per serving (~8.8 g GL); individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume in moderation and account for the carbohydrate contribution within their meal plan.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (7.8 g per 100 g), trans fat (0.2 g per 100 g), and sodium (512 mg per 100 g) from deep-frying and cheese
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Mozzarella cheese sticks are high in saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium, all of which are established risk factors for cardiovascular disease; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit consumption and prefer lower-fat, lower-sodium alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mozzarella cheese sticks are a popular Western fast-food snack consisting of elongated pieces of mozzarella cheese encased in a seasoned wheat-flour breadcrumb coating and deep-fried until golden and crispy on the outside while the cheese interior melts to a soft, stretchy, gooey texture. Each stick is typically 5–8 cm long and is served hot, often accompanied by a marinara or tomato dipping sauce. The dish originated in the United States and is widely served in pizza restaurants, fast-food chains, and casual dining establishments globally, including in Singapore where it appears on menus such as Now Pizza at Funan. A standard serving of 4 pieces weighs approximately 112 g and provides around 319 kcal, 15 g protein, 18 g fat (including approximately 8.7 g saturated fat), and 25 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is notable at approximately 574 mg per serving, making this a high-sodium snack. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 35 (low GI), reflecting the dominant protein and fat content of the mozzarella cheese core, though the breadcrumb coating contributes a moderate glycemic load. Clinically, this food is of concern for individuals managing hypertension and cardiovascular disease due to its high sodium and saturated fat content. For individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, the low GI is a positive attribute but portion control remains important. Mozzarella cheese sticks are a good source of calcium (~347 mg per serving) and provide moderate amounts of protein and vitamin A from the cheese component.
common_user_queries: How many calories in mozzarella cheese sticks?
How much sodium in mozzarella cheese sticks?
Are mozzarella sticks high in fat?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central data for breaded fried mozzarella sticks, which is an approved secondary global source. No Singapore-specific or restaurant-specific nutritional data is available for this exact product from Now Pizza at Funan. GI is an analogous estimate based on peer-reviewed literature. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Serving size of 4 pieces estimated at 112 g based on typical restaurant portion weights for mozzarella cheese sticks (each stick approximately 28 g). Vitamin C is 0.0 mg per 100 g; this is confirmed zero as mozzarella cheese and wheat breadcrumb coating contain negligible vitamin C, confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. Trans fat of 0.2 g per 100 g reflects residual trans fats from deep-frying in partially hydrogenated oils; actual value may vary by restaurant. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all populated from USDA FoodData Central adapted data; no sentinel -1 values are used as USDA provides these values for this food category. GI set to 35 as an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this exact product exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_100g × serving_size_g) / 10000 = (35 × 22.4 × 112) / 10000 = 8.8 g. Halal status is not confirmed for this restaurant product; is_halal set to false as default. FoodOn URI FOODON_03302114 is used as the best available match for mozzarella stick; if this URI is not confirmed in the current FoodOn release, foodon_match_type should be revised to closest_parent. FoodEx2 code A0CJW is used as closest parent for processed cheese product.

Set GI to 35 (analogous estimate) with full peer-reviewed source documentation.
Set gi_category to 'low' consistent with GI ≤ 55 threshold rule.
Documented vitamin_c_mg = 0.0 as confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central.

Verified aliases_by_language english entries all appear verbatim in flat aliases array.
Confirmed G6 multilingual rule does not apply to American dishes; local_names fields correctly set to null.
